clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Identify and manage resistant hypertension, including medication optimization and renal denervation candidacy
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Evaluation of patients with uncontrolled BP on ≥4 antihypertensives, or stage 2 hypertension not at goal despite optimal therapy
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Secondary cause evaluation → medication optimization (MRA) → alternative agents → RDN candidacy assessment

navigationPath.purpose: Review medications, check for interfering agents, evaluate for secondary causes

Screen for contraindications (neurogenic orthostatic hypotension, pregnancy, FMD, stented renal artery, renal artery aneurysm, significant renal artery stenosis, known kidney or secreting adrenal tumors)
Verify first-line therapy (ACEi/ARB + CCB + thiazide-like diuretic), check eGFR
If eGFR ≥45, add MRA (COR 1, LOE B-R)
If MRA intolerant/contraindicated, offer amiloride, BBs, alpha-blockers, central sympatholytics, dual endothelin receptor antagonists, or direct vasodilators (COR 2a, LOE B-NR)
Multidisciplinary team evaluation for RDN candidacy (COR 1, LOE B-NR)
Discuss BP lowering benefits vs procedural risks (COR 1, LOE C-EO)
Proceed with RDN as adjunct treatment if criteria met (COR 2b, LOE B-R)

currentMeds.validation: Must include ACEi/ARB + CCB + thiazide-like diuretic

validationRules: Resistant hypertension: office SBP ≥140 and DBP ≥90 on ≥4 meds including ACEi/ARB + CCB + thiazide-like diuretic
Stage 2 hypertension not at goal: SBP ≥140 and DBP ≥90 on optimal or near-optimal therapy

rules: Step 1: Identify resistant hypertension (office SBP ≥140 and DBP ≥90 despite ≥4 antihypertensives including ACEi/ARB + CCB + thiazide-like diuretic)
Step 2: Evaluate for secondary causes, review all medications, remove interfering agents (COR 1, LOE B-NR)
Step 4: If eGFR ≥45 and uncontrolled despite optimal first-line therapy, add MRA (COR 1, LOE B-R)
Step 5: If MRA intolerant/contraindicated, add alternative agent (amiloride, BBs, alpha-blockers, central sympatholytics, dual endothelin receptor antagonists, direct vasodilators) (COR 2a, LOE B-NR)
Step 6: For persistent uncontrolled hypertension or intolerable side effects, evaluate for RDN with multidisciplinary team (COR 1, LOE B-NR)
Step 7: Shared decision-making discussing BP benefits vs procedural risks (COR 1, LOE C-EO)
Step 8: If selected, RDN as adjunct treatment (COR 2b, LOE B-R)

categories.name: RDN Candidacy
categories.condition: resistant or intolerant to additional meds

rdnCandidate.condition: if resistant or intolerant to additional meds

thresholds.sbp: N/A - resistant hypertension is chronic management, not acute crisis

appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing through medication optimization and RDN evaluation

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Refer to multidisciplinary team with expertise in resistant hypertension and RDN for evaluation
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor BP after each medication adjustment; reassess for RDN if BP remains uncontrolled after optimal medical therapy
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm office BP measurements; consider ambulatory BP monitoring
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag patients with resistant hypertension for care team review; coordinate multidisciplinary evaluation
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log medication optimization steps, MRA trial outcomes, contraindications, shared decision-making discussion, RDN evaluation results
